lesson.menu_desc: Persuasion & Influence: sell a growth initiative with the second conditional and a sharp value proposition — 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 7 — Pitching with Impact — Persuasion, Influence & the Second Conditional
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Victor Malvezi Paschotto | Aula 7 — Pitching with Impact

listenings.text: Good morning, and thank you for the time. I want to put one idea on the table today: a regional self-service kiosk for our LATAM clients. Here is the value proposition. If we placed smart kiosks in our fifty busiest branches, we would cut transaction time in half and free up our staff for higher-value work. Picture this. A client walks in, completes a routine deposit in ninety seconds, and walks out, while our team focuses on the relationships that actually grow revenue. The bottom line is simple. If we invested two million now, we would see a return inside eighteen months, and the model is fully scalable to other regions. I would argue that this is not just a cost saving. It is a genuine game changer for how we serve the market. The momentum is already there. Three branch managers have asked for exactly this. So the real question is not whether we can afford to do it. It is whether we can afford to wait. I would appreciate your support to run a pilot in two branches this quarter.

Hi Victor, it is Sophie. I listened to your draft pitch and I have a few quick thoughts before the board meeting. First, your hook is strong, so lead with it. Open with the second conditional, not with background. If we placed kiosks in the busiest branches, we would cut wait times in half. That sentence does the work of three slides. Second, every claim needs a tangible number. The board does not buy adjectives, they buy evidence, so pair every benefit with a figure. Third, name the upside and the rationale in the same breath. If we ran the pilot, we would learn fast and we would lower the risk of a full rollout. And one more thing. When they push back on the cost, do not get defensive. Reframe it. If we delayed this, we would simply hand the advantage to a competitor. Keep the energy high and the ask small, a two-branch pilot, not the whole region. Land that and the rest follows. You have a real game changer here, so go and sell it. Let me know how it goes.
